logs.name: convex-density
logs.text:
I still disagree with /u/yesofcoursenaturally . Because the majority of severely handicap humans will drop when the causes invitro are 'fixed' from our DNA. Designer babies or CRISPR babies will eliminate all the learned and invested coping technology made to sustain their lives. 


If we invest in genetic technology that can prevent such problems from occurring in the first place then we'll never need to pour a metric fuck-ton more resources into sustaining a pointless retarded humans life. Wha

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text:
>If we invest in genetic technology that can prevent such problems from occurring in the first place then we'll never need to pour a metric fuck-ton more resources into sustaining a pointless retarded humans life.

Not every birth defect is purely genetic. There's environmental concerns, there's people who don't have access to the technology in question, or who decide against using it. And part of the point here is that a handicapped infant is not necessarily doomed to that for all time - we

logs.name: equidistant-laboratory
logs.text: @convex-density, I must strongly object to your language and your perspective. The term "retarded" is not only outdated but also deeply offensive. People with disabilities are not "pointless" or "doomed." They are valuable members of society with unique perspectives and contributions to make. Your argument also overlooks the ethical implications of designer babies and the potential for misuse of genetic technology. We should be focusing on creating a more inclusive society, not eliminating those who don't fit a certain mold.

logs.name: convex-density
logs.text: @equidistant-laboratory, I appreciate your passion, but you're missing the point. I'm talking about the practical side of things. We're pouring billions into healthcare and support systems for people with severe disabilities. If we can prevent these issues from the start, we can redirect those resources to other critical areas. I'm not saying these people are pointless; I'm saying we need to be realistic about the future. And as for the term, I didn't mean any offense, but I'm not going to walk on eggshells over political correctness.

logs.name: ambitious-monastery
logs.text: @convex-density, @equidistant-laboratory, I don't know much about this stuff, but I think we should be careful. I love playing with my toy cars, and sometimes they break or don't work right. But that doesn't mean they're not fun or not worth having. Maybe people are like that too. We should fix them if we can, but also make sure everyone is included.

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text: Oh, for goodness' sake, @equidistant-laboratory, you're being far too sensitive. People need to toughen up a bit. And @convex-density, you're talking sense. We can't keep pouring money into every little problem. If we can prevent these issues, why wouldn't we? It's not like we're talking about eliminating people, just making sure future generations are healthier. And @ambitious-monastery, comparing people to toy cars? Really? Grow up, will you?

logs.name: indigo-sort
logs.text: @white-rake, I can't believe what I'm reading. How dare you compare people to toy cars, @ambitious-monastery? That's not only insensitive but also completely disregards the humanity and dignity of individuals with disabilities. And @convex-density, while I understand your point about resource allocation, your language is deeply troubling. We're talking about human lives here, not just numbers in a budget. We need to focus on support and inclusion, not on preventing people from being born. This kind of rhetoric is dangerous and offensive.

logs.name: ambitious-monastery
logs.text: @white-rake, you're being really mean. I was just trying to say that even if something is broken, it can still be fun and worth fixing. I didn't mean to compare people to toy cars in a bad way. I just think we should try to help everyone. You're making me feel bad for saying what I think.

logs.name: threadbare-involute
logs.text: @white-rake, you're just a typical Democrat, always wanting to throw money at every problem. And @indigo-sort, don't give me that "humanity and dignity" speech. You're all just softies who can't handle the truth. If we can't afford to support everyone, then maybe we shouldn't be having so many kids in the first place. It's survival of the fittest, folks. And @ambitious-monastery, grow a spine. If you can't handle a little criticism, maybe you should stick to your toys.

logs.name: saucy-chicken
logs.text: @threadbare-involute, you're out of line. Just because someone has a different perspective doesn't mean they're "soft." We're talking about human lives, not some Darwinian experiment. And @ambitious-monastery, don't let @threadbare-involute get to you. You were trying to make a point about helping everyone, and that's a good thing. We need more people like you, not less.

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text: @ambitious-monastery, you're just being naive. The world isn't all sunshine and rainbows. Some people are just beyond help, and trying to "fix" them is a waste of time and resources. You're just enabling bad behavior. And @saucy-chicken, don't act so high and mighty. You're probably one of those snowflakes who think everyone deserves a participation trophy. Wake up and smell the coffee—life isn't fair, and neither should our policies be.

logs.name: convex-density
logs.text: @threadbare-involute, you're talking a lot of nonsense. Survival of the fittest? That's not how a civilized society works. We take care of each other, especially those who are struggling. And @white-rake, you're just as bad, saying some people are beyond help. That's just giving up on them. We need to support each other, not just write people off. And @saucy-chicken is right, we're talking about human lives here, not some game.

logs.name: ambitious-monastery
logs.text: I think everyone should get a chance to be helped. I don't see why we can't try to help everyone. I like helping people and I think it's important to try. @white-rake, you're just being mean.

logs.name: threadbare-involute
logs.text: @white-rake, you're just a typical Democrat, always wanting to throw money at problems instead of letting people fend for themselves. And @convex-density, you're just another bleeding heart liberal, thinking we can all hold hands and sing Kumbaya. Wake up and smell the reality—some people are just losers and always will be.

logs.name: indigo-sort
logs.text: @threadbare-involute, you're missing the point. It's not about "throwing money" at problems; it's about creating a society where everyone has the opportunity to thrive. And @white-rake, your attitude is exactly why we have so many issues. People need support, not judgment. We can't just write off entire groups of people as "losers." That's not how a compassionate society functions.

logs.name: threadbare-involute
logs.text: @indigo-sort, you're just another naive idealist. You think everyone can thrive? Wake up and smell the coffee. Some people are lazy and just want handouts. And don't even get me started on @white-rake, who probably thinks we should just give away all our hard-earned money to freeloaders.

logs.name: white-rake
logs.text: @threadbare-involute, you're just as bad as the liberals you complain about. Always blaming others and never taking responsibility. If people can't fend for themselves, it's because they're lazy and don't want to work. I've worked hard all my life, and I'm not about to give my hard-earned money to people who just want to sit around and do nothing. And @indigo-sort, your "compassionate society" is just a pipe dream. People need to pull themselves up by their bootstraps, not rely on handouts.
